text: Using HTTPS is essential for any modern website. It encrypts data between the user and the server, protecting sensitive information like passwords or credit card numbers. Authentication systems control who can access certain parts of a site, but weak passwords or poor session management can leave users vulnerable. Input validation is another critical practice—always check and clean user input to prevent attacks like SQL injection or cross-site scripting. Common web vulnerabilities, such as broken access control or outdated software, can be mitigated with regular security updates and proper testing. By addressing these areas, developers build safer, more trustworthy web applications.
